channel.title: Telemetry on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Telemetry on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: Reconstructing AI activity in investigations
item.description:
Microsoft published a playbook for investigating AI activity in Microsoft 365 Copilot and Azure AI services using telemetry. It focuses on reconstructing events, assessing data exposure, and speeding threat detection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Ingest OTLP signals into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description:
Azure Monitor now generally supports native ingestion of OTLP signals. OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications and platforms can send telemetry directly to Azure Monitor.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluate feature rollouts with Azure App Configuration Scorecards
item.description:
Azure App Configuration has added scorecards in public preview. The feature uses telemetry to show how feature flag variants are performing in production so teams can assess rollout impact.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub CLI: Opt-out usage telemetry
item.description:
GitHub CLI v2.91.0 adds pseudonymous usage telemetry by default. The release notes say the data is used to improve the product and is opt-out.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description:
Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available. It provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation for secure, high-throughput, enterprise-scale scenarios.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Incident response for AI: Same fire, different fuel
item.description:
Microsoft published a blog post on incident response for AI, focusing on how existing IR practices still apply while AI-specific telemetry, tools, and skills are needed. It frames AI incidents as similar to traditional response work but with different signals and operational needs.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics now includes organization-level GitHub Copilot CLI activity. Organization admins can view CLI-specific activity and usage totals in 1-day usage metrics, completing telemetry coverage across enterprise, user, and organization levels.

  • Source: GitHub
